content_markdown: The fundamental (first harmonic) frequency for a particular organ pipe is $330\text{ Hz}$. The pipe is closed at one end but open at the other. What is the frequency of its next highest harmonic?

diagram_metadata.description: No diagram provided in the image.

vibe_explanation: For a pipe closed at one end and open at the other, only odd harmonics are present ($f_n = n f_1$ where $n = 1, 3, 5, \dots$). The fundamental frequency is $f_1 = 330\text{ Hz}$. The next highest harmonic is the third harmonic ($n=3$), so the frequency is $3 \times 330\text{ Hz} = 990\text{ Hz}$.
